_comment: Vikings guild layer for 3s - and the DESIGN CHECKPOINT for the
per-layer config format (spec 5 / open items). Every section a
guild file can carry is demonstrated below. The cascade rule is
REPLACE: an entry here with the same key as a mud/global entry
supplants it; a character-file entry supplants this one.
Sections:
login_commands - sent once, on the first MIP vitals packet
(i.e. when actually logged in). Whole-list
replace: defining it here replaces any list
from a lower layer.
aliases        - dict, key = alias name
triggers       - list of {pattern, command, sound?, mode?};
key = pattern. mode: party|noparty
gags           - list of regex strings, merged as a set
keys           - keysym -> command (see global.json for the
numpad defaults this can override)
vitals         - whole-list replace: bar row layout
mip            - tag -> handler declaration; replaces the
mud-layer declaration for the same tag
mip_required   - field self-diagnosis (spec 5.1): if 'field'
never appears within 'within' seconds of
combat activity, a ONE-TIME warning naming
the field and the hint appears in the
status pane (never the main output)
map_patches    - guild-scope graph patches (spec 6.2), e.g.
the guild exit from a clan house
settings       - per-key replace of any client setting
Fill in real Vikings content as the character develops; the
entries below are working examples, not placeholders that
break anything if left in place.

settings._post_kill_resume_note: vrest (appended to the corpse cascade below) has no completion line to wait on, unlike bladesinger revalrie - so the hold is vitals-gated instead of text-gated: every bot engine (Bot/Hunt/Run) waits until hp/seid/vig/rad are all within post_kill_resume_vitals_margin of max, or the timeout.
